SEARCH
Package com.cellsimulation.app

Class MainController

java.lang.Object
com.cellsimulation.app.MainController
All Implemented Interfaces:
SimulationListener
public class MainController extends Object implements SimulationListener
JavaFX controller of the main application window.

Acts as the glue between the FXML view and the simulation model: it owns a SimulationEngine, attaches a StatisticsService for the live chart, and uses a SaveService to persist or restore a simulation through the File menu.

The controller also implements SimulationListener so that the canvas, the population counters and the chart are refreshed automatically after every tick. A JavaFX Timeline drives the simulation cadence (no custom thread is started); each key-frame simply asks the engine to play one tick, which in turn fires the listener callbacks that update the UI.

The naming of onResetButton() (rather than onReset) is deliberate: the listener callback onReset() carries the same signature as the toolbar handler would, so the toolbar handler was renamed to avoid a compile-time clash. The two methods serve different purposes: the toolbar handler pauses the engine and asks for a reset, the listener callback merely refreshes the UI after the engine has reset itself.

  • Constructor Details

    • MainController

      public MainController()

  • Method Details

    • initialize

      public void initialize()
      Called by the JavaFX runtime after the FXML view has been loaded and every fx:id-annotated control has been injected.

      Builds the default 20x20 bounded engine, attaches a fresh StatisticsService and the controller itself as listeners, primes the chart series, populates every control with its default value, creates the playback Timeline and wires the dynamic listeners (sliders, combobox, canvas click, canvas resize).

      The order is deliberate: defaults are written to the controls before the slider listeners are attached, so the initial setValue calls do not echo back into SimulationSettings mutators.

    • onTick

      public void onTick(Grid grid, int tickCount)
      Listener callback invoked by the engine after each tick.

      Redraws the grid, refreshes the population counters and appends the latest snapshot to the four chart series.

      Specified by:
      onTick in interface SimulationListener
      Parameters:
      grid - the current grid (unused here: this controller reads the grid from the engine via SimulationEngine.getGrid())
      tickCount - the tick number reported by the engine

    • onReset

      public void onReset()
      Listener callback invoked by the engine when it is reset.

      Wipes the chart, redraws the (now empty) grid and refreshes the counters. The toolbar reset action is implemented in onResetButton().

      Specified by:
      onReset in interface SimulationListener
